Research Vault Dashboard

Generated: 2026-03-21 · 3 of 12 slots · 77% deployed · 23% cash

Ras Laffan LNG 17% Capacity Destroyed — Structural Supply Deficit (Day 21)
Iran struck Qatar Ras Laffan with ballistic missiles. 17% LNG export capacity destroyed, 3-5yr repair (QatarEnergy CEO confirmed). Multi-state Gulf infrastructure attacks: Saudi Yanbu bypass, Kuwait 800K b/d, UAE Habshan. Brent $113.77/$119 intraday. Both sides destroying production facilities. GTT D1/D3/D8 upgraded; CF D6/D7 strengthened.
CF Conviction Upgraded 7.5 → 8/10 — Cluster A 80%
D2 (Valuation Mismatch) confirmed 4/5 after NOLA $673-675/st breached $636/st trigger. Sell-side consensus $7.65 EPS still not reflecting S3. Next gate: April Tampa NH3 settlement.
GTT Allocation Doubled 10% → 20% — Two Adds on Mar 21
Added 5% at 10→15% (situation inflecting: Ras Laffan structural + FID acceleration confirmed), then 5% at 15→20% (adding into market weakness). Cash 33%→23%. Deployed 67%→77%. GTT CWA 0.80→1.60. Portfolio total CWA 5.76→6.56.
DOJ Antitrust Probe — CF Price-Fixing Investigation
DOJ investigating CF, Nutrien, Mosaic, Koch, Yara for potential fertilizer price-fixing (~82% US nitrogen capacity). Civil AND criminal under review. Two class-action farmer lawsuits filed. Grand jury (criminal) = material risk; civil = manageable. Monitor Q1 10-Q legal disclosures (May).
USDA Prospective Plantings — Mar 31 (10 days)
Corn >93M acres = demand intact; <92M = volume headwind 10%+. Market pricing 94M acres. Binary demand signal for CF (17% position).

Hard date. Corn >93M acres = demand intact; <92M = volume headwind. Market pricing 94M. Position 17%.

Conviction vs. Allocation (Investments)
Exposure Analysis
Geopolitical (37%)
Day 21+: Hormuz closed; both sides destroying production facilities. Ras Laffan 17% LNG destroyed (3-5yr repair). Yanbu bypass struck. Brent $113.77. S3 base case 55%. No ceasefire mechanism. GTT Hormuz correlation only ~3-4% of YTD move — structural, not geopolitical.
Micro-Execution (77%)
VOW (40%): Q1 2026 May 20 — ~68% new-contract mix. CF (17%): NOLA $673-675/st confirmed above S3 trigger. Tampa NH3 imminent. USDA Mar 31. GTT (20%): Q1 activity update Apr 22; consensus gap €740-780M vs €902.8M. CP2 $8.6B financing + Ksi Lisims/Cedar FID acceleration + Qatar rebuild ~40-50 carriers.
Macro (37%)
FOMC holding 3.50-3.75%; PCE forecast raised to 2.7%. Oil $113+ risks CPI re-acceleration → rate cuts priced out. 10Y at 4.27% (up 25bps). India Kharif demand Apr-Jun. Inflation re-acceleration risk as second-order headwind for all names.
Market Structure
VOW: zero sell-side coverage. CF: consensus EPS $13.54 still not reflecting S3; named #1 S&P 500 performer (+45% in 60 days) — momentum crowding risk. GTT: Ras Laffan structural LNG deficit bridging FY2026-27 trough — ceasefire no longer fully unwinds thesis. Consensus gap €740-780M vs €902.8M.

STABLE
25/25
Impact: 5/5 Discovery: 5/5 Deployed: 40%
Thesis: VOW SOTP implies +212% upside (NOK 7.54 vs 2.42) with imminent hard catalyst (Q1 results May 20, Q2 Aug 2026).
Scenarios: Deep Bear 10% (NOK 1.12) · Base 35% (NOK 5.76) · Bull 40% (NOK 9.01) · Super Bull 15% (NOK 12.06)
Archive Trigger: VOW re-rates to NOK 4-6 OR covenant breach invalidates thesis
Latest Signal: War-driven risk-off continues suppressing small-cap sentiment globally, reinforcing the mispricing mechanism. Norwegian Krone under pressure from oil volatility.

Hormuz Disruption
STABLE
20/25
Impact: 5/5 Discovery: 4/5 Deployed: 37%
Thesis: Day 21+ — qualitative escalation to infrastructure destruction. Ras Laffan 17% LNG destroyed (3-5yr repair). Both sides now destroying production facilities. S3 base case (3-month, $800-1000/t urea) at 55%.
Scenarios: S1 3% (2wk) · S2 15% (4wk) · S3 55% (3mo) · S4 27% (6mo)
Archive Trigger: Ceasefire + confirmed Hormuz reopening + mine clearance timeline
Latest Signal (Mar 20 eve): Permission-based transit model evolving (Pakistan, India, Turkey selective passage) but negligible volume. Iran using Kooh Mobarak bypass. Brent-WTI spread $16-18 (11yr high). JKM $22.35. Methanol 35-45% Hormuz-dependent. Fertilizer “most under-appreciated” commodity. No ceasefire: Iran FM explicitly rejects.

Briefing Feed (Recent Signals)
▶
Mar 20 eve — Hormuz Evening Briefing
Permission-based transit evolving (Pakistan, India, Turkey). Methanol 35-45% Hormuz-dependent (validates MEOH). Brent-WTI $16-18 (11yr high). Fertilizer “most under-appreciated.” No new candidates.
Mar 20 — Hormuz Daily Briefing + Israel-Iran War Report
Day 21: infrastructure destruction phase. Ras Laffan 17% LNG destroyed (3-5yr). Multi-state Gulf attacks. Brent $113.77/$119. Copper archived. GTT upgraded TRADE→INVESTMENT 8/10.
Mar 19 — Hormuz Daily Briefing
All 5 themes STABLE. Commodity pricing confirms S3: urea +35-45%, ammonia +7-46%, LNG +85%. EU CBAM $75/t ammonia surcharge. CF conviction 7.5→8/10.
Mar 18 — Daily Intel + Hormuz Commodity Briefing
Leadership decapitation; Iran rejects ceasefire; India 22 vessels partial passage; Shah gas field suspended; S3 maintained 55%
Mar 17 — Financial News Report + Hormuz Evening
2 new candidates (EU Defense, Private Credit). Hormuz bypass targeting (Fujairah hub, oil storage, UAE gas). 120 bcm/yr LNG disrupted.
Mar 16 — Q4 Earnings + Daily Intel + Houthi Assessment
Hidden Value SOTP promoted. S3 upgraded to 55%, S4 to 25%. Dual chokepoint NOT active. NOLA $645/st breached $600 checkpoint 4 days early.
